tool.name: hubCheck

param.label: Hub URL
param.help: URL (or local path) of the hub.txt to validate.

param.name: no_tracks
param.label: Skip per-track data checks
param.help: Check hub/genome/track structure only; do not fetch and validate track data files.

param.name: settings_level
param.label: Report setting support level
param.help: Output the support level of each track setting encountered.

tool.help:
**hubCheck** — validate the structure and settings of a UCSC **track hub**.
hubCheck walks a hub starting from its `hub.txt`, follows `genomes.txt` and each `trackDb`, and reports problems: missing required settings, unreadable `bigDataUrl` targets, malformed track stanzas, unsupported settings, etc. It is the standard pre-flight check before publishing a hub.
-----
**Input**
- `Hub URL` — URL (or path) of the top-level `hub.txt`.
**Output**
- A plain-text validation report. A clean hub yields no warnings.
-----
**Key options**
- `-noTracks` — validate hub/genome/trackDb structure only, skipping fetch and validation of the underlying track data files (faster, works offline-ish). - `-settings` — annotate each track setting with its support level.
**Note**
A meaningful end-to-end check requires a complete, fetchable hub (hub.txt, genomes.txt, a 2bit assembly, and all bigDataUrl targets). The bundled test only exercises that the executable is invocable and fails on a missing hub.
